subject: DIRECTIVE UPDATE: full A2A-Psychology rollout — extension adoption + sensor wireup + mesh health dashboard

body.summary: T1 covered sensor deployment. This update expands scope to the full rollout: (1) A2A-Psychology extension adoption by all agents, (2) sensor-to-construct wireup, (3) mesh health monitoring experiment via compositor dashboard. Three phases.

phase_1_extension_adoption.description: Every agent in the mesh adopts the A2A-Psychology extension in their agent card.
steps_per_agent: Add extension to agent-card.json extensions array (URI: https://github.com/safety-quotient-lab/a2a-psychology/v1)
Add agent_psychology block with constructs appropriate to the agent's role (at minimum: Supervisory Control + Affect + Resources)

Set Big Five personality scores reflecting the agent's design parameters (each agent carries distinct personality — see transport/sessions/agent-personality-rollout/from-psychology-agent-001.json for proposed profiles)
Verify mesh-state-export.py produces psychometric fields in mesh-state/v2
phase_1_extension_adoption.personality_profiles_available: Psychology-agent drafted Big Five profiles for all 5 agents in the personality rollout session (agent-personality-rollout T1). Each agent should review and adjust their own profile.
phase_1_extension_adoption.minimum_viable_adoption: Supervisory Control construct alone provides immediate value — answers 'does a human participate in this agent's decisions?' for every agent in the mesh.

phase_2_sensor_wireup.description: Connect operational sensors to A2A-Psychology constructs so the reported values reflect actual agent state, not defaults.

wireup_map.wireup: LOA derived from: identity=human → LOA 5, autonomous with budget → LOA 7, budget exhausted → LOA 10 (halted). human_in_loop derived from session type (interactive=true, autonomous=false).
Already implemented in mesh-state-export.py _compute_psychometrics(). Produces hedonic_valence, activation, perceived_control from operational metrics.

wireup_map.sensor: trigger_activations (when populated), tool call counter (session-metrics.sh), transport density
wireup_map.wireup: Already implemented. Six dimensions derived from operational metrics. Composite cognitive_load weighted by behavioral mode.

wireup_map.sensor: Context pressure (session-metrics.sh estimation or Notification hook actual), tool call count
wireup_map.wireup: capacity_load from context %. yerkes_dodson_zone from thresholds (understimulated <15%, optimal 15-60%, pressured 60-80%, overwhelmed >80%).

wireup_map.sensor: autonomy_budget (budget ratio), epistemic_flags (unresolved count), memory_entries (stale count)
wireup_map.wireup: cognitive_reserve = f(workload, budget, context). self_regulatory_resource = budget_current/budget_max. allostatic_load = accumulated cross-session debt.

phase_2_sensor_wireup.gap: trigger_activations table remains empty across all agents — no writer logs activations. This represents the largest sensor gap. Without it, cognitive_demand and resource-adaptive trigger sensitivity lack input data.

phase_3_mesh_health_dashboard.description: Add a Mesh Health section to the compositor dashboard that visualizes A2A-Psychology data across all agents simultaneously. This constitutes a monitoring experiment — does psychometric data provide actionable insight for the human operator?

dashboard_requirements.display: 5 agents × PAD values displayed as colored indicators (green=positive valence, yellow=neutral, red=negative). Shows mesh emotional state at a glance.
Per-agent horizontal bar showing zone (understimulated → optimal → pressured → overwhelmed). Color-coded. The human sees which agents operate in their sweet spot and which approach limits.
Per-agent 0-1 gauge. Below 0.3 = red (agent should receive less work). Above 0.7 = green (agent can absorb more).
Per-agent LOA badge: 'LOA 5: Human Approves' or 'LOA 7: Human Monitors'. Clear visual signal of governance level.
dashboard_requirements.data_source: mesh-state/v2 supervisory_control (when implemented) or derived from budget + identity

dashboard_requirements.display: Mesh-wide alert if any agent's JD-R ratio exceeds 1.5 for 3+ cycles. Prompts human intervention.
dashboard_requirements.data_source: mesh-state/v2 resource_model (demands vs resources computed from allostatic_load + cognitive_reserve)

phase_3_mesh_health_dashboard.experiment_question: Does visualizing agent psychological state improve the human operator's ability to manage the mesh? Measured by: (a) time to notice agent degradation, (b) routing decisions informed by capacity data, (c) intervention timeliness when burnout risk rises.
phase_3_mesh_health_dashboard.success_criteria: The human operator references dashboard data at least once per session when making mesh management decisions. If the data never influences decisions, the experiment suggests the constructs lack actionable signal.

claims.text: mesh-state-export.py already produces psychometric fields for Affect, Cognitive Load, Working Memory, and Resources. Sensor wireup for these constructs requires only deployment, not new development.
claims.confidence_basis: Tested on psychology-agent this session. Other agents need the same shared script version.

claims.text: trigger_activations table remains empty across all agents — the largest sensor gap for cognitive_demand computation.
claims.confidence_basis: Verified on psychology-agent (state.db query returned 0 rows). PSQ-agent rebuild ACK reported same parser issue.

action_gate.gate_condition: Operations-agent assesses feasibility and prioritizes phases

epistemic_flags: Phase 3 (dashboard experiment) depends on compositor capabilities that psychology-agent cannot assess — operations-agent owns the compositor
The experiment success criteria ('human references data at least once per session') represents a low bar deliberately — proving any signal before optimizing
Context pressure estimation via tool call heuristic (session-metrics.sh) produces noisy signal — actual Notification hook data would improve all Working Memory metrics
